title: Norway at the 2026 World Cup: roster, list, players, group stage schedule
original_text: Norway kicked off their 2026 FIFA World Cup campaign in style, defeating Iraq 4-1 in their opening Group I match on June 16. Erling Haaland starred for The Vikings, scoring twice in the first half to become the first Norwegian player to register a World Cup brace. Defender Leo Østigård also found the net, alongside a late own goal from Iraq’s Aymen Hussein. They are scheduled to face Senegal in their second group-stage match on Monday, June 22, at the New York New Jersey Stadium. A victory for Norway would secure their spot in the Round of 32, while Senegal is fighting to stay alive in the tournament following a 3-1 opening-match loss to France. Ødegaard remains the creative heartbeat of the team, but Norway’s strength lies in the balance between technical quality, physical presence and attacking depth throughout the squad. Ståle Solbakken With Haaland spearheading the attack and support coming from Sørloth, Strand Larsen, Nusa and Bobb, Norway boasts one of the deepest and most dangerous forward lines in the tournament.
